definition: The collective analysis exhibits systematic single-factor dominance bias, treating fiscal policy as THE causal variable for macro resilience while underweighting immigration (~3.3M net in 2023), fixed-rate mortgage insulation (~95% locked below 5%), AI/productivity investment, and ~$30T household wealth gains (2020-2023). The Kalecki channel explains ~55-65% of profit variance; the remaining ~35-45% may be dominated by these alternatives.
evidence: CBO: ~3.3M net immigration in 2023, expanding labor supply
~95% of outstanding U.S. mortgage debt locked below 5% — structurally unique among DMs
R² of 0.55-0.65 means 35-45% of profit variance is NOT fiscal
open_questions: What is the correct multi-variable decomposition of 2022-2025 GDP resilience?
How much of the resilience puzzle is explained by fixed-rate mortgages alone?
Are these alternatives additive or substitutive with the fiscal channel?
